2007-05-01.title: Sunni Ministers Threaten to Quit Cabinet in Iraq
2007-05-01.abstract: Members of Parliament’s largest Sunni bloc are frustrated over the government’s failure to act on Sunni concerns.

2007-05-01.title: Olmert Rebuked by Israeli Panel on Lebanon War
2007-05-01.abstract: Prime Minister Ehud Olmert suffered a further blow today when a Labor minister resigned over the report.

2007-05-01.title: Qaeda Leader May Have Been Killed, Iraqi Official Says
2007-05-01.abstract: Iraqi authorities are investigating reports that the alleged leader of Al-Qaeda in Mesopotamia, Abu Ayyub al-Masri, has been killed in a struggle within his own group, the interior ministry said Tuesday.

2007-05-01.title: Ivica Racan,  Former Croatian Premier, Is Dead at 63
2007-05-01.abstract: While prime minister of Croatia, Mr. Racan rebuilt economic and trade ties with Europe following the civil wars of the 1990s.

2007-05-01.title: Philippine Police Close Case in American’s Killing
2007-05-01.abstract: Juan Duntugan, a woodcarver, admitted on television and in a written statement to the police that he killed Julia Campbell, 40, from Fairfax, Va., in a fit of rage while both were walking along a trail.

2007-05-02.title: 3 Climate Change Envoys Appointed
2007-05-02.abstract: Secretary General Ban Ki-moon chose three international figures to help him chart a way for the United Nations to take the lead in addressing climate change. The special envoys are Gro Harlem Brundtland, a former prime minister of Norway; Ricardo Lagos, a former president of Chile; and Han Seung-soo, a former foreign minister of South Korea and president of the General Assembly in 2001. Michèle Montas, Mr. Ban’s spokeswoman, said the three would prepare proposals for the opening of the General Assembly in September when national leaders come to New York and for a United Nations conference in Bali, Indonesia, in December. Last month, the first-ever debate on climate change was held in the Security Council.

2007-05-02.title: U.S. Jailer in Iraq Admits Mistakes, Investigator Says
2007-05-02.abstract: A commander in the main U.S. detention center lent prisoners his cellphone and used public funds to supply Saddam Hussein with hair dye, investigators claimed.

2007-05-02.title: As Funding Increases, Afghan Forces Range From Ragtag to Ready
2007-05-02.abstract: Efforts to create a capable Afghan Army and a police force have been accompanied by a mix of achievements and frustrations.

2007-05-02.title: Former World Bank Officials Detail Discord Over Wolfowitz
2007-05-02.abstract: Two officials disputed the contention of Paul D. Wolfowitz, the bank president, that they knew about his efforts to arrange a pay raise and promotion for his companion.

2007-05-02.title: Chávez Takes Over Foreign-Controlled Oil Projects in Venezuela
2007-05-02.abstract: The move by President Hugo Chávez is the centerpiece of recent actions aimed at consolidating his government’s control over the economy.

2007-05-03.title: Friction Between Estonia and Russia Ignites Protests in Moscow
2007-05-03.abstract: Protesters in Russia disrupted a news conference by the Estonian ambassador, harassed diplomats and briefly blocked trucks crossing the border near St. Petersburg.

2007-05-03.title: Democracy in Nigeria Falters but Is Far From Dead
2007-05-03.abstract: Eight years into civilian government after a long spell of military dominance, Nigeria’s institutions are blossoming despite the recent electoral chaos.

2007-05-03.title: Israel Reveals New Details of Allegations Against Ex-Lawmaker
2007-05-03.abstract: Azmi Bishara, an Israeli Arab suspected of passing information to Hezbollah during the war waged against the Lebanese militia, could be charged with treason.

2007-05-03.title: Protesters Gather to Urge Olmert to Resign
2007-05-03.abstract: The protests follow a damning report on Ehud Olmert’s leadership during Israel’s war with Lebanon.

2007-05-03.title: Iraqi Blocs  Opposed to Draft Oil Bill
2007-05-03.abstract: Kurdish and Sunni Arab officials are concerned over a draft of a bill establishing a framework for the distribution of oil revenues.

2007-05-04.title: Olmert Holds on for Now, Despite Huge Public Outcry
2007-05-04.abstract: More than 100,000 protesters gathered in the first mass demonstration since the war in Lebanon, which many Israelis saw as a failure.

2007-05-04.title: China Now Testing Food for Chemicals
2007-05-04.abstract: Regulators are testing for substances that are believed to have killed or sickened thousands of pets in the United States.

2007-05-04.title: Wolfowitz Rebuts Critics Over Tempest at World Bank
2007-05-04.abstract: Paul D. Wolfowitz said that he had helped arrange a generous pay and promotion package for his companion as “a settlement of her potential claims” if she sued for being relocated against her will.

2007-05-04.title: U.S. Says Its Troops Killed a Top Iraqi Insurgent Who Aided in the Kidnapping of Westerners
2007-05-04.abstract: American officials denied claims from Iraqi officials that other leaders of the insurgent group Al Qaeda in Mesopotamia had been killed.

2007-05-04.title: Clinton Proposes Vote to Reverse Authorizing War
2007-05-04.abstract: Senator Hillary Rodham Clinton’s proposal sharpens her own political positioning by confronting the White House.

2007-05-05.title: Scottish Nationalists Upset Labor in Elections
2007-05-05.abstract: The separatist Scottish National Party emerged as the biggest political grouping in Scotland, redrawing Britain’s political landscape.

2007-05-05.title: Ukraine’s Quarreling Leaders Agree on Early Elections
2007-05-05.abstract: The announcement seemed to be a victory for President Viktor A. Yushchenko, who had earlier ordered that Parliament be disbanded and called for new elections.

2007-05-05.title: Carlos the Jackal to Be Tried for Role in 4 Bombing Attacks in ’80s
2007-05-05.abstract: The cold-war-era terrorist known as Carlos the Jackal, already serving a life sentence, will be tried in connection with four bombing attacks in France in 1982 and 1983.

2007-05-05.title: In French Bid, Immigrant’s Son Battles Reputation as Anti-Immigrant
2007-05-05.abstract: Nicolas Sarkozy, the possible next president of France, has done as much, if not more, than any other French official to improve the status of minorities.

2007-05-05.title: Venezuela: A Chill Follows Threat by Chávez
2007-05-05.abstract: Financial markets reacted with concern to a threat by President Hugo Chávez to nationalize the banking system. Mr. Chávez said Thursday that banks should finance industry at a low cost. “If banks don’t agree with this, it’s better that they go, that they turn over the banks to me, that we nationalize them and get all the banks to work for the development of the country and not to speculate and produce huge profits,” Mr. Chávez said. Venezuela’s currency, the bolívar, fell more than 3 percent in unregulated trading to about 3,950 to the dollar, traders said, and investors sold government bonds amid fears that a state takeover of banks could weaken the economy.

2007-05-06.title: Propaganda Fear Cited in Account of Iraqi Killings
2007-05-06.abstract: Documents suggest that senior U.S. officers saw civilian deaths in Haditha as a problem requiring a misleading response.

2007-05-06.title: Invisible Palestinians Exist in Legal Limbo in Lebanon
2007-05-06.abstract: Generations of families are not allowed to graduate from school, legally marry, or hold a job, or even set foot outside of their rundown camps.

2007-05-06.title: Iraqi Courts Seek to End Sunni’s Immunity
2007-05-06.abstract: The move could clear the way for the lawmaker’s arrest on charges of provoking sectarian violence.

2007-05-06.title: French Voters Pick Sarkozy to Be President; Turnout High
2007-05-06.abstract: Nicolas Sarkozy earned a decisive victory today over Ségolène Royal, keeping the right in power for another five years.  Turnout was estimated at 85 percent of France’s registered voters.

2007-05-06.title: France Heads to the Polls After a Scorching Finale
2007-05-06.abstract: France began voting for president today. Nicolas Sarkozy led Ségolène Royal 55 to 45 in a poll released late Friday.

2007-05-07.title: Olmert Survives Three No-Confidence Motions
2007-05-07.abstract: At least 16 members of Ehud Olmert’s governing coalition voted against the Israeli prime minister, abstained or were absent.

2007-05-07.title: Throngs Attend Speech by Pakistan’s Suspended Justice
2007-05-07.abstract: The chief justice of Pakistan’s supreme court was suspended by the government after he investigated some of its practices.

2007-05-07.title: Clash at U.N.-Run Gaza School Wounds 6
2007-05-07.abstract: The chief of the United Nations Relief and Works Agency in Gaza, John Ging, who was inside during the fighting, said two of those wounded were schoolchildren.

2007-05-07.title: Panel to Find That Wolfowitz Broke Rules, Officials Say
2007-05-07.abstract: World Bank officials said the committee investigating misconduct charges against Paul D. Wolfowitz would eventually find that he violated bank rules barring conflicts of interest.

2007-05-07.title: Afghan Soldier Said to Have Shot U.S. Military Trainers
2007-05-07.abstract: KABUL, Afghanistan, May 7 — The man who shot and killed two American military trainers on Sunday, and wounded two others, was a soldier of the Afghan National Army, the Afghan Ministry of Defense confirmed today.

2007-05-08.title: In World Skyscraper Race, It Isn’t Lonely at the Top
2007-05-08.abstract: Designed to be the world’s tallest building, the Shanghai World Financial Center has been superseded by taller structures across Asia.

2007-05-08.title: Sarkozy Sets a New Course for Relations With U.S.
2007-05-08.abstract: In his acceptance speech, Nicolas Sarkozy signaled his desire to end tension with Washington.

2007-05-08.title: Germany: No Pardons for 2 in Terrorism Gang
2007-05-08.abstract: President Horst Köhler turned down a politically contentious request for clemency by a terrorist from the far-left Red Army Faction, also known as the Baader-Meinhof gang. Mr. Köhler met last week with the man, Christian Klar, who has served 24 years of a life sentence for the murder of a German prosecutor in 1977. The meeting drew protests from conservative politicians, who said Mr. Klar had shown no remorse for his crimes. Speculation that he might be freed rose after a fellow gang member, Brigitte Mohnhaupt, was released in March after serving 24 years in prison for murders in the 1970s. The president also denied clemency to another member, Birgit Hogefeld.

2007-05-08.title: China Tells Little About Illness That Kills Pigs, Officials Say
2007-05-08.abstract: The lack of basic details revives questions about whether China is willing to share information about health and food safety issues.

2007-05-08.title: Suicide Bombers Kill 25 Near Ramadi, Dealing Blow to Recent Gains Against Insurgents
2007-05-08.abstract: Pressure is building on Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki to move forward on legislation to help salvage a rift in the Iraqi government.

2007-05-09.title: Treasury Chief Supports Delay for Wolfowitz
2007-05-09.abstract: Treasury Secretary Henry M. Paulson Jr. endorsed the request by Paul D. Wolfowitz, the World Bank president, for more time to defend himself against charges of misconduct.

2007-05-09.title: Clinton Foundation Announces a Bargain on Generic AIDS Drugs
2007-05-09.abstract: Former President Bill Clinton also endorsed recent decisions by Thailand and Brazil to break patents held by American pharmaceutical companies.

2007-05-09.title: House Democrats Push Plan to Finance Iraq War in Stages
2007-05-09.abstract: The plan would provide $30 billion for combat operations through midsummer, but require a second round of Congressional votes in July.

2007-05-09.title: Rights Groups Object to Serbia’s Leading Council of Europe
2007-05-09.abstract: The objections come at a time when Belgrade is in violation of the international Genocide Convention.

2007-05-09.title: As Pope Heads to Brazil, Abortion Debate Heats Up
2007-05-09.abstract: A dispute broke out today between the Roman Catholic Church and the Brazilian government about abortion.

2007-05-10.title: Cheney Visits Baghdad and Presses Leaders on Political Progress
2007-05-10.abstract: Vice President Dick Cheney told Iraqis that political progress in Baghdad is essential if U.S. military support is to be sustained.

2007-05-10.title: Afghans Say U.S. Airstrikes Killed 21 Civilians
2007-05-10.abstract: The airstrikes were the latest in a series of claims of noncombatant casualties that have strained relations with the Afghan government.

2007-05-10.title: The Netherlands: Stiffer Sentence for Iraq Poison Gas
2007-05-10.abstract: An appeals court raised the 2005 prison sentence of a Dutch businessman, Frans van Anraat, to 17 years from 15 for selling chemicals to Saddam Hussein. The chemicals were used in poison gas weapons in the 1980s and against Kurdish villagers. Prosecutors had demanded a conviction for complicity in genocide, but the appeal judges rejected that, saying Mr. van Anraat was driven not by genocidal intent but by greed.

2007-05-10.title: Mubarak Meets Israel’s Foreign Minister
2007-05-10.abstract: CAIRO, Egypt, May 10 — President Hosni Mubarak met with Israel’s foreign minister, Tzipi Livni, today and agreed that a team of Arab officials would soon visit Israel to discuss a peace proposal initiated by Saudi Arabia and endorsed by all members of the Arab League.

2007-05-10.title: Olmert’s Testimony on War Is Made Public
2007-05-10.abstract: The Israeli prime minister said that the killing of civilians in Qana was a turning point that delayed a resolution.

2007-05-11.title: Blair Endorses Gordon Brown as His Successor
2007-05-11.abstract: Mr. Brown planned a busy campaign to persuade Britons of his suitability for the job of prime minister.

2007-05-11.title: Republicans in Congress Press Bush for Sanctions on Sudan
2007-05-11.abstract: The group demanded that President Bush impose sanctions against the Sudanese government for its failure to rein in the violence.

2007-05-11.title: Ramos-Horta Wins East Timor’s Presidential Election
2007-05-11.abstract: HONG KONG, May 11 — José Ramos-Horta won a decisive victory in East Timor’s presidential election this week, a significant move toward creating genuine multi-party democracy in the country and breaking the dominance of the political organization that largely led its struggle for independence, analysts and election observers said today.

2007-05-11.title: U.N. Security Council to Review Draft on Kosovo Independence
2007-05-11.abstract: The plan is favored by ethnic Albanians but it is fiercely opposed by Serbia and its veto-bearing ally on the Council, Russia.

2007-05-11.title: Shots Fired at Residence of Aide to Pakistan’s Ousted Chief Judge
2007-05-11.abstract: The attack raised fears of an escalation of the tension and violence since March when President Pervez Musharraf suspended the Chief Justice.

2007-05-12.title: 5 Dead After Attack on U.S. Patrol in Iraq; 3 Still Missing
2007-05-12.abstract: Seven U.S. soldiers and an Iraqi army interpreter came under coordinated attack today in a Sunni stronghold south of Baghdad.

2007-05-12.title: East Timor Figure’s Victory Is Seen as Cause for Hope
2007-05-12.abstract: The presidential election of Mr. Ramos-Horta, the prime minister and a Nobel Peace Prize laureate, was a significant move toward creating genuine multi-party democracy.

2007-05-12.title: Canada: Court Delays Terrorism Suspect’s Deportation
2007-05-12.abstract: The Federal Court said the government may not deport an Algerian accused of having ties to Al Qaeda until it revised its law on detaining foreign-born terrorism suspects. The Algerian, Mohamed Harkat, was arrested in Ottawa in 2002 and became one of five Muslim men held without charges or a trial under the detention measure, the security certificate system, introduced late in 2001. In February, the Supreme Court found the measure unconstitutional and gave the government a year to change it. Mr. Harkat was released under restrictive conditions after the ruling as the government continued his deportation process.

2007-05-12.title: A Congressman Faces Foes in Japan as He Seeks an Apology
2007-05-12.abstract: Michael M. Honda's resolution on Japan’s wartime sex slavery would provide justice to surviving women who were drafted into Japanese military brothels.

2007-05-12.title: No Tribunal for 11 Officers Tied to Killing of Brazilian in Britain
2007-05-12.abstract: The police said that they mistook Jean Charles de Menezes for a terrorist, who was fatally shot one day after an alleged failed terrorist attack.

2007-05-13.title: Civilian Deaths Undermine Allies’ War on Taliban
2007-05-13.abstract: Deaths from American and allied airstrikes are threatening popular support for the Afghan government and straining the NATO alliance.

2007-05-13.title: Russia to Get Central Asian Pipeline
2007-05-13.abstract: The deal delivers a major setback to continuing American efforts to send Central Asian natural gas exports directly to Europe.

2007-05-13.title: Immigrants Reject Quebec’s Separatists
2007-05-13.abstract: Quebec’s growing number of immigrants points to a shift that is forcing political parties, separatist and federalist, to rethink their political foundations.

2007-05-13.title: Pakistani Leader Assailed for Deadly Clashes
2007-05-13.abstract: ISLAMABAD, Pakistan, May 13 — A day after political clashes claimed 39 lives in Karachi, political analysts said the violence — and accusations that the government had done little to stop the killings — had badly damaged the Pakistan’s president, Gen. Pervez Musharraf.

2007-05-13.title: Amid Friction, Plans for U.S.-Iran Talks on Iraq
2007-05-13.abstract: Ambassadors will meet in Baghdad to discuss Iraqi security efforts and cooperation between the countries.

2007-05-14.title: Bank Files May Undercut Wolfowitz, Critics Say
2007-05-14.abstract: Testimony and notes from the vice president of human resources at the World Bank were “devastating” to Paul D. Wolfowitz’s case, according to a bank official.

2007-05-14.title: Violence Reported in Philippines Elections
2007-05-14.abstract: Voters went to the polls in the Philippines today to elect senators, congressmen and local officials, amid reports of election-related violence and allegations of fraud.

2007-05-14.title: On the Canals, a Woman Paddles Against the Tide
2007-05-14.abstract: For more than a thousand years, Venice had gondolas but never a female gondolier. Now there is Alexandra Hai.

2007-05-14.title: Antiwar Iraqi in Washington Has a More Sectarian Agenda at Home
2007-05-14.abstract: Muhammad al-Daini, a Sunni Arab member of the Iraqi Parliament, has offered a well-practiced pitch that emphasizes the need for American troops to withdraw.

2007-05-14.title: Search for 3 G.I.’s Abducted in Iraq Continues
2007-05-14.abstract: The Al Qaeda group that claimed responsibility for the attack warned that the soldiers would never be found.

2007-05-15.title: At Least 7 Killed in Gaza Fighting
2007-05-15.abstract: The violence in Gaza threatened to further undermine the Palestinians’ fragile “unity” government.

2007-05-15.title: Nigeria: Election Body Must Produce Evidence, Court Says
2007-05-15.abstract: An election tribunal ruled that the government must produce evidence that supports its declaration of a winner in last month’s presidential election, delivering an early victory to efforts to challenge the balloting in court. A tribunal judge said that electoral officials must allow Atiku Abubakar, the vice president and third-place finisher in the presidential contest, to examine the ballot papers, tally sheets and other materials used in declaring Umaru Yar’Adua, the candidate of the governing party, the winner. A European Union observer mission declared the voting “not credible,” and Nigerian observers demanded a rerun. Mr. Yar’Adua is set to be inaugurated on May 29.

2007-05-15.title: Britain: Blair and Bush to Meet
2007-05-15.abstract: Prime Minster Tony Blair plans to visit Washington this week and meet with President Bush to discuss issues related to the Group of 8 meeting in Germany next month. Mr. Blair’s spokesman, who customarily speaks in return for anonymity, said the discussions would also cover climate change, Iraq and the Middle East. The Washington trip is being billed as part of Mr. Blair’s farewell schedule before he leaves office on June 27.

2007-05-15.title: U.S. Soldier Is Shot Dead in Ambush in Pakistan
2007-05-15.abstract: The shooting occurred after a meeting between Afghan, Pakistani and NATO officials at the border to try to resolve recent clashes between the neighboring countries.

2007-05-15.title: Central Asians Chase Jobs, and Endure Exploitation
2007-05-15.abstract: The number of Uzbek migrants crossing into Kazakhstan has spiked in response to  economic vitality in Kazakhstan and Russia and worsening conditions elsewhere in Central Asia.

2007-05-16.title: Doubts Arise on How a Cricket Coach Died
2007-05-16.abstract: The latest twist in the police investigation into the death of Bob Woolmer, the Pakistani cricket coach, may be that he was not murdered at all.

2007-05-16.title: Hamas Attacks Against Fatah Kill 14 and Add to Gaza Chaos
2007-05-16.abstract: Members of the military wing of Hamas attacked Palestinian Authority security forces loyal to Hamas’s rival, Fatah, killing nine, according to Fatah officials.

2007-05-16.title: After Rice and Putin Meet, Russia Agrees to Soften Language
2007-05-16.abstract: Cordiality seemed to be restored, but the United States and Russia remained at an impasse on the issues of missile defense and the status of Kosovo.

2007-05-16.title: Lebanon: U.S. to Introduce Resolution for U.N. Tribunal
2007-05-16.abstract: Zalmay Khalilzad, the American ambassador to the United Nations, said a resolution would be introduced this week calling on the Security Council to create a tribunal to judge the killers of Rafik Hariri, the former prime minister who was assassinated in a Beirut car bombing in 2005. The action was in response to a request from Fouad Siniora, the current prime minister, whose government is locked in a struggle with pro-Syrian forces that are against an international court. A United Nations investigation has linked the assassination to the Syrian security services. Saying he endorsed the decision to act now, Secretary General Ban Ki-moon said, “I am of the view that there should be no impunity for the perpetrators of political assassinations.”.

2007-05-16.title: Documents From Vast Nazi Archive to Be Made Available to Scholars
2007-05-16.abstract: An agreement could speed access to electronic copies of documents about millions of people who were victimized during and after the Nazi period.

2007-05-17.title: Land Wars in Venezuela
2007-05-17.abstract: President Hugo Chavez's ambitious land reforms have injected the poor with optimism, and brought dread to the wealthy.

2007-05-17.title: Sarkozy Picks Fillon as Prime Minister
2007-05-17.abstract: France’s new president hopes the appointment  will smooth the introduction of his ambitious reform program.

2007-05-17.title: Iraqis Admit Ambush Roles as U.S. Searches for 3 G.I.’s
2007-05-17.abstract: Military officials detained several local residents and recovered equipment that might have belonged to the missing American soldiers.

2007-05-17.title: Street Battles in Iraqi Cities Point to Dire Security Status
2007-05-17.abstract: On a day of wide-ranging violence that underscored the grave security situation across much of Iraq, militia gunmen and Iraqi security forces fought openly.

2007-05-17.title: Clash of Hope and Fear as Venezuela Seizes Land
2007-05-17.abstract: President Hugo Chávez is redistributing his country’s farmland  in a process that is both brutal and legal.

2007-05-18.title: Germany: Ex-C.I.A. Detainee Held in Arson Attack
2007-05-18.abstract: The police arrested Khaled el-Masri, the German citizen of Lebanese descent who says he was subjected to the Central Intelligence Agency’s practice of extraordinary rendition, on suspicion of setting a fire at a market in the Bavarian town of Neu-Ulm that caused an estimated $675,000 in damage, his lawyer said. A judge ordered him held in the psychiatric ward of a local hospital. The lawyer, Manfred Gnjidic, said in a telephone interview that Mr. Masri, who says he was seized while on vacation in Macedonia in 2003 and flown to Afghanistan, where he was questioned about ties to terrorist groups and beaten, had had a dispute with the store and that his action was a result of not having received the psychological counseling he had sought. “I have asked several times from the beginning for psychological help, but without any luck,” Mr. Gnjidic said. “The very ironic thing is that he will get psychological help now, after he committed a crime, and as a torture victim didn’t get it before, when he asked for it.”.

2007-05-18.title: Hunt for 3 G.I.’s in Iraq Slowed by False Trails
2007-05-18.abstract: Thousands of soldiers, sifting through hundreds of tips, are searching for the three missing Americans.

2007-05-18.title: Mosque Bombing Kills Seven in India
2007-05-18.abstract: A crude bomb exploded during Friday prayers at a 17th-century mosque in Hyderabad, a major city in southern India.

2007-05-18.title: Violence Continues in Gaza
2007-05-18.abstract: Fighting between Palestinian factions and rocket exchanges between Israel and Hamas continued today.

2007-05-18.title: Army Punished 2 Officers in ’06 After Failures in Iraq Ambush
2007-05-18.abstract: A platoon leader and a company commander were relieved of their commands for their shortcomings in leadership.

2007-05-19.title: Eviction Notice Is Latest Russian Move Against Journalists
2007-05-19.abstract: Critical of the Kremlin’s curtailment of the independent news media, the largest society of independent journalists in Russia received an eviction notice on Friday.

2007-05-19.title: British Girl Vanishes in Portugal, and Her Country Grieves
2007-05-19.abstract: The disappearance of Madeleine McCann on the cusp of her fourth birthday has absorbed Britons, touching the nation’s sense of pity and outrage.

2007-05-19.title: Sarkozy’s Top Diplomat: Undiplomatic Opposite
2007-05-19.abstract: Bernard Kouchner, named as France’s new foreign minister, earned his reputation by challenging authority.

2007-05-19.title: Estonia Computers Blitzed, Possibly by the Russians
2007-05-19.abstract: Estonian officials declared that their country is the first to fall victim to cyberwarfare.

2007-05-19.title: 2 Activists Are Under House Arrest and Barred From Leaving China
2007-05-19.abstract: China barred an AIDS and human rights activist and his wife from leaving the country and placed them under house arrest.

2007-05-20.title: Bombs Imperil U.S. Troops Searching for Captured Comrades
2007-05-20.abstract: American soldiers are encountering bombs buried far from roads that strike them as they fan out to cover Iraq’s rural terrain.

2007-05-20.title: Lebanese Troops Clash With Islamic Militants
2007-05-20.abstract: The battle left at least 22 soldiers and 17 militants dead and set off a military campaign to root out militants inside Lebanon’s Palestinian refugee camps.

2007-05-20.title: Israel Strikes Home of Palestinian Lawmaker
2007-05-20.abstract: The attack came hours after Israel announced that it would intensify military operations in Gaza.

2007-05-20.title: U.S. Force Kills Shiite Linked to Deaths of 5 Soldiers
2007-05-20.abstract: The suspect, Sheikh Azhar al-Duleimi, was killed in a firefight after American troops raided a house in northern Baghdad.

2007-05-20.title: Ghosts Wail as Cambodians Plunder Killing Field Graves
2007-05-20.abstract: Some 200 graves of victims of the Khmer Rouge had been dug up and the bones scattered through the woods by local villagers hunting for jewelry.

2007-05-21.title: Anthony M. Brooks, Undercover Foe of Nazis, Dies at 85
2007-05-21.abstract: As a British undercover agent, Mr. Brooks led a network of saboteurs who delayed German reinforcements for weeks after the Normandy invasion.

2007-05-21.title: Birth Control Measures Prompt Riots in China
2007-05-21.abstract: A campaign to enforce strict population-control measures prompted rioting in southwestern China in recent days.

2007-05-21.title: Electricity Crisis Hobbles an India Eager to Ascend
2007-05-21.abstract: Amid the hum of industry and a roaring economy, half of India’s people are left off the power grid.

2007-05-21.title: One-Ounce Belgian Idols Vie for Most Tweets Per Hour
2007-05-21.abstract: In the 400-year-old Flemish competition known as vinkensport, winning finches are feted like feathered opera divas.

2007-05-21.title: Dozens Slain as Lebanese Army Fights Islamists
2007-05-21.abstract: The crisis was one of the most significant challenges to the army since the end of Lebanon’s bloody civil war.

2007-05-22.title: Iran Accuses American of Revolution Plot
2007-05-22.abstract: The Islamic Republic of Iran accused a prominent American academic it imprisoned two weeks ago of conspiring to foment a velvet revolution there.

2007-05-22.title: Baghdad District Is a Model, but Only for Shiites
2007-05-22.abstract: In Kadhimiya, which offers Iraqis relative safety, religious Shiite leaders and their militias have consolidated control.

2007-05-22.title: Cocaine Wars Make Port Colombia’s Deadliest City
2007-05-22.abstract: As larger cities have calmed, Buenaventura has emerged as the deadliest urban center in Colombia’s internal war.

2007-05-22.title: Attacks in Sunni Areas in Baghdad Kill at Least 29
2007-05-22.abstract: Today’s attacks, a car bomb and mortar shells in Sunni neighborhoods in Baghdad, also wounded 85 people.

2007-05-22.title: Fighting in Lebanon Dashes Cease-Fire Hopes
2007-05-22.abstract: The Lebanese Army continued battling militants holed up in a refugee camp on the outskirts of Tripoli.

2007-05-23.title: Israelis Don’t Want Gaza to Be Their Next Lebanon
2007-05-23.abstract: While the Hamas militants in Gaza seem to have taken a lesson from last summer’s inconclusive war against the rockets of Hezbollah, Israel’s own lesson is less clear.

2007-05-23.title: China Arrests 28 on ‘Instigating Riots’ Charges
2007-05-23.abstract: Police in southwestern China arrested 28 people on charges that they instigated riots last weekend over family planning controls.

2007-05-23.title: Bombing Kills Six in Turkish Capital’s Shopping District
2007-05-23.abstract: Eight Pakistani officials were among the more than 70 injured in the Ankara attack.

2007-05-23.title: Furious Street Battles Remind Lebanon of Its Past
2007-05-23.abstract: The first clear look inside a refugee camp on Tuesday revealed the hellish outcome of a three-day battle.

2007-05-23.title: Russia: Mine Officials Who Turned a Blind Eye to Safety Are Fired
2007-05-23.abstract: The authorities dismissed five mine safety officials for allowing a Siberian coal mine to “break safety rules for the benefit of profit,” an action they said led to an explosion in March that killed 108 miners, the biggest mine disaster in Russia’s recent history. An investigation showed that the mine’s managers deliberately disabled a methane gas detector. A buildup of methane, sparked by a short circuit, caused the blast. The dismissals, reported by the Interfax news agency, were an exception to the usual blanket impunity enjoyed by government officials.

2007-05-24.title: Refugees Pour Out of Camp in Lebanon
2007-05-24.abstract: About 15,000 Palestinians left the Nahr al Bared refugee camp as the Lebanese Army reinforced its vow to crush a militant Palestinian group inside.

2007-05-24.title: Mine Blast Kills at Least 38 in Siberia
2007-05-24.abstract: The explosion opened fresh wounds in a community scarred by an accident two months ago that claimed more than 100 lives.

2007-05-24.title: Brazil: Minister Quits Under Cloud
2007-05-24.abstract: In a widening corruption scandal, the sixth to strike the government of Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva since he took office in 2003, the minister of mines and energy, Silas Rondeau, resigned. Citing wiretapping and videotape evidence, the police say he accepted a $50,000 bribe from a construction company in return for preferential treatment in the awarding of contracts for a rural electric program.

2007-05-24.title: Turkey Vows to Pursue Militant Kurds Into Iraq
2007-05-24.abstract: Turkish authorities said they had identified the suicide bomber in an attack on Tuesday in Ankara as a man from a predominantly Kurdish city.

2007-05-24.title: After Gains in the North, Sinn Fein Seeks a Louder Voice in Ireland
2007-05-24.abstract: Sinn Fein, the political affiliate of the Irish Republican Army, is hoping to gain seats in the government in the elections on Thursday.

2007-05-25.title: Shiite Cleric Resurfaces With Anti-U.S. Sermon
2007-05-25.abstract: After a four-month sojourn, Moktada al- Sadr today delivered a sermon calling for U.S. forces to leave Iraq.

2007-05-25.title: Algeria’s Women Quietly Advance in Careers and Society
2007-05-25.abstract: In a tradition-bound nation, women are emerging as an economic and political force unheard of in the rest of the Arab world.

2007-05-25.title: Iraqi Shiite Cleric Reportedly Ends a Sojourn in Iran
2007-05-25.abstract: Moktada al-Sadr has quietly returned to Iraq after four months in Iran, according to U.S. intelligence reports.

2007-05-25.title: Ukrainian Political Crisis Escalates
2007-05-25.abstract: President Viktor A. Yushchenko’s decree putting interior troops under his command was called unconstitutional.

2007-05-25.title: Ireland Votes on Whether to Give the Prime Minister a Third Term
2007-05-25.abstract: Prime Minister Bertie Ahern has said the election will probably be his last before he steps down from politics in five years.

2007-05-26.title: Russia and Britain Spar Over Poison Case
2007-05-26.abstract: The top prosecutors of Russia and Britain quarreled over a British demand to extradite a former K.G.B. security officer accused of poisoning Alexander V. Litvinenko in London last November. After a meeting of justice officials from the Group of 8 countries in Munich, Russia’s prosecutor general, Yuri Y. Chaika, said the suspect, Andrei K. Lugovoi, could face trial in Russia, provided the British provide “evidence proving Lugovoi’s guilt,” but he reiterated Russia’s refusal to extradite him. The British attorney general, Peter Goldsmith, responded that British justice should decide the matter. “This murder was committed on U.K. soil,” he said in a statement. “The evidence is in the U.K. A U.K. citizen was killed and other people put at risk. And it is therefore right a suspect should face justice in a U.K. court.”.

2007-05-26.title: Battle Over Control of Troops Escalates Political Crisis in Ukraine
2007-05-26.abstract: President Viktor A. Yushchenko’s decree in putting Ukraine’s Interior Ministry troops under presidential command has escalated a political crisis.

2007-05-26.title: Shiite Cleric Ends Absence From Iraq With Fiery Speech
2007-05-26.abstract: Moktada al-Sadr, appearing in public for the first time in months, demanded a timetable for American forces to withdraw from Iraq.

2007-05-26.title: Within 25 Miles of New York, Iran Offers a Congenial Glow
2007-05-26.abstract: Javad Zarif, the United Nations ambassador from Iran, a country that has had no diplomatic relations with the United States since 1980, is confined in New York.

2007-05-26.title: Lawmakers in Colombia Urge Firing of Mediator
2007-05-26.abstract: Allegations surfaced that an area set aside for peace talks, which a foreign diplomat helped oversee, turned into the party ground for outlawed Colombian paramilitaries.

2007-05-27.title: Strife Foreseen in Iraq Exit, but Experts Split on Degree
2007-05-27.abstract: There is fear of a violent chain reaction should U.S. troops withdraw, but opinions differ as to how bad things would get.

2007-05-27.title: Ukraine Rivals Agree on Election, Defusing Crisis
2007-05-27.abstract: The political season before new elections will assuredly reflect the divisions that have bedeviled the country.

2007-05-27.title: Olmert Warns of More Strikes on Hamas
2007-05-27.abstract: Israel’s prime minister vowed to continue the assault on Hamas as an Israeli man was killed by a rocket launched from Gaza.

2007-05-27.title: Doubts Grow as G.I.’s in Iraq Find Allies in Enemy Ranks
2007-05-27.abstract: The gung-ho attitude of many U.S. soldiers has now turned to disdain as they face increased attacks from the same Iraqi Army forces they helped to train.

2007-05-27.title: Bombs Kill 2 G.I.’s in Iraq as Death Toll Near Year’s High
2007-05-27.abstract: American forces began running DNA tests on a body that could be one of the remaining two missing soldiers abducted in an ambush on May 12 south of Baghdad.

2007-05-28.title: Militants Widen Reach as Terror Seeps Out of Iraq
2007-05-28.abstract: The Iraq war, which for years has drawn militants from around the world, is beginning to export fighters and tactics to neighboring countries and beyond.

2007-05-28.title: Irish Classic Is Still a Hit (in Calfskin, Not Paperback)
2007-05-28.abstract: The Book of Kells, the splendidly illustrated Gospels in Latin written 1,200 years ago, is about to undergo laser analysis.

2007-05-28.title: 3 Afghan Employees of French Aid Group Freed by the Taliban
2007-05-28.abstract: The three men, held hostage for seven weeks by Taliban insurgents, were freed unharmed after local residents intervened.

2007-05-28.title: Militant Hints at Resolution in Lebanon
2007-05-28.abstract: Statement on Sunday from a spokesman for the Fatah al Islam militant group offered hope that the standoff could end peacefully.

2007-05-28.title: Facing Inquiry, Japanese Official Commits Suicide
2007-05-28.abstract: The agricultural minister, Toshikatsu Matsuoka, was to face parliamentary questioning on a political scandal.

2007-05-29.title: Kazakhstan Issues Warrant for President’s Son-in-Law
2007-05-29.abstract: The warrant for Rakhat Aliyev, one of the country’s most powerful men, is the latest scandal involving violence and corruption to captivate political life in Kazakhstan.

2007-05-29.title: Chief Justice of Pakistan Says Musharraf Pressed Him to resign
2007-05-29.abstract: Iftikhar Muhammad Chaudhry also said he was detained all afternoon against his will at the Army House in Rawalpindi.

2007-05-29.title: Rise in Violence in North Shows Afghanistan’s Fragility
2007-05-29.abstract: Supporters of Gen. Abdul Rashid Dostum clashed with the police, and a suicide bomber attacked a convoy of foreign security contractors.

2007-05-29.title: China Sentences Former Drug Regulator to Death
2007-05-29.abstract: The unusually harsh sentence comes at a time of concerns for the quality and safety of China’s food and drug system.

2007-05-29.title: Fears Linger in Mozambique Over Unexploded Weapons
2007-05-29.abstract: Since a disastrous blast at a munitions depot in Maputo, Mozambique on March 22, some residents fear that the depot will again explode due to government neglect.

2007-05-30.title: Interrogation Methods Are Criticized
2007-05-30.abstract: A set of experts is calling harsh techniques used since the 2001 terrorist attacks outmoded, amateurish and unreliable.

2007-05-30.title: Ex-Chief of China Food and Drug Unit Sentenced to Death for Graft
2007-05-30.abstract: The former director of China’s top food and drug safety agency was sentenced to death on Tuesday after pleading guilty to corruption and accepting bribes.

2007-05-30.title: Ex-Trade Envoy Is Bush’s Choice for World Bank
2007-05-30.abstract: Robert B. Zoellick, a Goldman Sachs executive, has been tapped to try to heal rifts from Paul D. Wolfowitz’s ouster.

2007-05-30.title: Russia Hails Test of a New Missile It Says Can Pierce Defense Shields
2007-05-30.abstract: Russia held a test launching of a new intercontinental ballistic missile and said it had successfully fired the weapon almost entirely across its northern airspace.

2007-05-30.title: Palestinian and Israeli Leaders to Meet Next Week, as Tensions Remain High
2007-05-30.abstract: Mahmoud Abbas and Ehud Olmert will continue a Washington-sponsored dialogue that will likely focus on another round of Israeli-Palestinian warfare.

2007-05-31.title: Meeting to Take Place in Palestinian Territories
2007-05-31.abstract: The meeting between Mahmoud Abbas and Prime Minister Ehud Olmert is expected to take place in the Palestinian territories for the first time, aides said.

2007-05-31.title: Colleagues Honor Slain Russian Journalist With a Book and Demand Justice
2007-05-31.abstract: The independent Russian newspaper Novaya Gazeta began sales of a collection of articles and commentary by a correspondent who was killed last year.

2007-05-31.title: Pakistan: 4 Arrested in Killing of Judge’s Aide
2007-05-31.abstract: The police arrested four men in the killing of Syed Hammad Raza, who worked as the chief of staff to Pakistan’s suspended chief justice. The interior minister, Aftab Ahmed Khan Sherpao, said the four belonged to a gang of robbers from Kashmir and shot Mr. Raza when they broke into his house in Islamabad on May 14. The police, who said they had recovered the gun used in the shooting and a stolen watch, maintained from the start that the killing was robbery-related. Mr. Raza’s wife denied there had been a robbery attempt and termed the shooting politically motivated because of Mr. Raza’s close association with the suspended justice, Iftikhar Mohammad Chaudhry.

2007-05-31.title: Israel: Peres Seeks a Chance to Serve Again
2007-05-31.abstract: Shimon Peres, a leading figure of Israeli political life, said he would again ask Parliament to elect him president, a symbolic and largely powerless post. Mr. Peres, 83, is supported by his latest party, the centrist Kadima, but he may not win the secret ballot on June 13. In a shock, Mr. Peres, left, lost in the last election, in July 2000, to Moshe Katsav, 61, a relatively unknown Likud politician who is ending his term accused of rape and sexual misconduct. Other candidates are Colette Avital from the Labor Party and Reuven Rivlin of Likud. “The president does not have much authority,” Mr. Peres said. “But he does have the authority to do good deeds.”.

2007-05-31.title: U.S. and Iraqi Forces Seek Abducted Britons in Raid
2007-05-31.abstract: U.S. and Iraqi troops raided Sadr City on Wednesday, looking for five British citizens abducted a day earlier from a nearby government building.
